title: YARA scan result ingestion and match alerting
dataSources: YARA scanner JSON/CEF forwarded to Splunk
spl:
index=yara sourcetype=yara:scan
| search match="true" OR match_count>0
| table _time, host, rule_name, path, match_count, scanner_version
| sort - _time
description: Ingests YARA scanner output and alerts on any match or severity threshold.
value: Ingests YARA scanner output and alerts on any match or severity threshold.
implementation: Normalize field names to your Splunk ES version and index names. Use summary indexing or ES introspection where `detection_metrics` is not present. For YARA, deploy a scripted input or HEC to ingest scanner JSON.
knownFalsePositives: YARA hits from authorized security research samples (EICAR, malware lab files), incident response forensics, or detection rule testing.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom TA / HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: YARA scanner JSON/CEF forwarded to Splunk.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize field names to your Splunk ES version and index names. Use summary indexing or ES introspection where `detection_metrics` is not present. For YARA, deploy a scripted input or HEC to ingest scanner JSON.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=yara sourcetype=yara:scan
| search match="true" OR match_count>0
| table _time, host, rule_name, path, match_count, scanner_version
| sort - _time
```

Understanding this SPL

**YARA scan result ingestion and match alerting** — Ingests YARA scanner output and alerts on any match or severity threshold.

Documented **Data sources**: YARA scanner JSON/CEF forwarded to Splunk. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom TA / HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: yara; **sourcetype**: yara:scan. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Detection type** for this use case: Operational metrics — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=yara, sourcetype=yara:scan. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Pipeline stage (see **YARA scan result ingestion and match alerting**): table _time, host, rule_name, path, match_count, scanner_version
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare sample matches in Splunk to the **YARA scan log** (or the scanner’s own report) for the same path, hash, and rule name.



Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Time chart, Single value

Scripted input (generic example)
This use case relies on a scripted input. In the app's local/inputs.conf add a stanza such as:

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/YourApp/bin/collect.sh]
interval = 300
sourcetype = your_sourcetype
index = main
disabled = 0
```

The script should print one event per line (e.g. key=value). Example minimal script (bash):

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Output metrics or events, one per line
echo "metric=value timestamp=$(date +%s)"
```

For full details (paths, scheduling, permissions), see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md
grandmaExplanation: We wire YARA match lines into the SIEM and alert on real hits so file-level malware sweeps are not an offline spreadsheet.
